notes: Kish identity k_eff(k,ρ) = k/(1+ρ(k-1)) as a definition; boundary theorems k_eff_at_zero, k_eff_at_one.

Ceiling k_eff → 1/ρ as k → ∞ (theorem) + directly-measured k_eff across 6 substrates (bench: k_eff sits in bounded band ≈7±2; measured max 9.7 at k=200; empirical corroboration of the ceiling as a saturation, not a growth).
Formal upper bound k_eff ≤ 1/ρ + ε past sufficiently large K (theorem k_eff_bounded_above).

Gate-0 discriminator on raw C. elegans whole-brain calcium (Kato 2015, 12 worms, up to 151 neurons): β = 0.10 ± 0.02, 95% CI excludes criticality band. Effective rank 1-3 across all 12 worms. Metric: PR-subsampling exponent β.
Second-substrate replication (Drosophila EPG compass, Mussells Pires 2024, N=32 ROIs × 180 fly-trials): β = 0.122 ± 0.077, 95% CI [0.110, 0.133]. Independent phylum, same low-dimensional side.

Direct-read k_eff vs k across 6 substrates (k = 21→200): k_eff ∈ [~5, 9.7], bounded band ≈ 7±2. Natural-substrate ceiling ≈ 10-11 consistent with 1/ρ_lower = 10 (Kish ceiling at the lower corridor bound).
Corridor (ρ_lower, ρ_upper) = (0.1, 0.43) is a well-formed open interval (theorem).
Asymptotic k_eff range inside the corridor is (1/ρ_upper, 1/ρ_lower) ≈ (2.33, 10). Lean theorem; bench-corroborated by measured k_eff ≤ 9.7.
Definition of the corridor membership predicate; foundation for the collapse/preservation theorems that use it as a hypothesis.
dρ/dt = α(ρ, S) - γ·M(t) as a definition — the two-pole dynamical law over-which the corridor sits as an attractor.
At M(t) = 0, dρ/dt > 0 (theorem): ρ drifts monotonically toward the rigidity pole. Structural fact underwriting F-10 (audit-pressure necessity).
The corridor is sustained only by non-trivial M(t) (theorem). Chaos-pole and rigidity-pole are both attractors under drift; the corridor is not.
The chaos pole ρ → 0 is entered under negative drift regimes (theorem). Explicit lower-pole exit dynamics.

k_eff is intrinsic to the substrate, not to the driving process: measured driver-correlation r ≈ −0.009 across driver-swap trials (raw values from coherence-ratchet driver-invariance sub-experiment; RATCHET/experiments carries the CCA-validation harness that produced the k_eff extractions used by the driver-invariance analysis).
